name: push
context: Push and pull Docker images to and from container registries including Docker Hub, GHCR, and ECR
info: This sub-skill covers pushing Docker images to container registries and pulling them for deployment. Registries are the distribution mechanism for container images. Docker Hub is the default public registry. GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) integrates with GitHub repositories and Actions. Amazon ECR provides private registries in AWS. Authentication is handled via docker login or credential helpers. Image references follow the format registry/repository:tag. Pushing requires tagging the image with the full registry path first. Pulling retrieves images by tag or digest. This sub-skill focuses on the push/pull operations themselves; see [[docker/registry]] for registry configuration and management, and [[docker/images/build]] for building images before pushing.
body:
# Pushing and Pulling Docker Images

Pushing uploads a locally built image to a container registry for distribution. Pulling downloads an image from a registry to a local machine for running as a container. These operations are the bridge between building and deploying.

## Authentication

Before pushing, authenticate with the target registry:

```bash
# Docker Hub
docker login
# Prompts for username and password/access token

# GitHub Container Registry (GHCR)
echo "$GITHUB_TOKEN" | docker login ghcr.io -u USERNAME --password-stdin

# Amazon ECR
aws ecr get-login-password --region us-east-1 | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin 123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com

# Google Artifact Registry
gcloud auth configure-docker us-docker.pkg.dev

# Azure Container Registry
az acr login --name myregistry
```

For CI/CD, use credential helpers or environment variables instead of interactive login. Store tokens as secrets in your CI system.

## Pushing Images

Images must be tagged with the full registry path before pushing:

```bash
# Tag for Docker Hub (default registry)
docker tag myapp:latest username/myapp:v1.0.0
docker push username/myapp:v1.0.0

# Tag and push to GHCR
docker tag myapp:latest ghcr.io/org/myapp:v1.0.0
docker push ghcr.io/org/myapp:v1.0.0

# Tag and push to ECR
docker tag myapp:latest 123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/myapp:v1.0.0
docker push 123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/myapp:v1.0.0

# Push all tags for a repository
docker push --all-tags username/myapp
```

### Push with Buildx

Buildx can build and push in a single command, avoiding the tag-then-push workflow:

```bash
# Build and push in one step
docker buildx build --push -t ghcr.io/org/myapp:v1.0.0 .

# Build for multiple platforms and push
docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 --push -t ghcr.io/org/myapp:v1.0.0 .
```

## Pulling Images

```bash
# Pull by tag
docker pull nginx:1.25-alpine

# Pull by digest (immutable, exact content)
docker pull nginx@sha256:abc123def456...

# Pull from GHCR
docker pull ghcr.io/org/myapp:v1.0.0

# Pull from ECR
docker pull 123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/myapp:v1.0.0

# Pull and immediately run
docker run -d -p 8080:80 ghcr.io/org/myapp:v1.0.0
```

## Multi-Platform Images

Modern deployments target multiple CPU architectures (amd64, arm64). Docker manifests list images for each platform:

```bash
# Create a multi-platform builder
docker buildx create --name multiplatform --driver docker-container --use

# Build and push multi-platform image
docker buildx build \
  --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
  --push \
  -t ghcr.io/org/myapp:v1.0.0 .

# Inspect manifest list
docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/org/myapp:v1.0.0
```

Docker automatically pulls the correct platform variant when running on any architecture.

## CI/CD Push Patterns

### GitHub Actions

```yaml
- name: Login to GHCR
  uses: docker/login-action@v3
  with:
    registry: ghcr.io
    username: ${{ github.actor }}
    password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

- name: Build and push
  uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
  with:
    push: true
    tags: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:${{ github.sha }}
```

### GitLab CI

```yaml
build:
  image: docker:24
  services:
    - docker:24-dind
  script:
    - docker login -u $CI_REGISTRY_USER -p $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD $CI_REGISTRY
    - docker build -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA .
    - docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA
```

## Registry Comparison

| Registry | Domain | Auth Method | Visibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| Docker Hub | `docker.io` | Username + token | Public/private |
| GHCR | `ghcr.io` | GitHub PAT or GITHUB_TOKEN | Public/private |
| Amazon ECR | `*.dkr.ecr.*.amazonaws.com` | AWS IAM | Private |
| Google AR | `*-docker.pkg.dev` | gcloud auth | Public/private |
| Azure ACR | `*.azurecr.io` | az acr login | Private |

See [[docker/registry]] for detailed registry configuration and lifecycle management.

sections.title: Registry authentication
sections.body: Authenticate with docker login for Docker Hub, echo token | docker login ghcr.io for GHCR, aws ecr get-login-password for ECR, gcloud auth configure-docker for Google, and az acr login for Azure. In CI/CD, use credential helpers or pipe tokens from environment secrets. Never hardcode credentials.

sections.title: Pushing images
sections.body: Tag images with the full registry/repository:tag path before pushing. Use docker push to upload layers. Docker only pushes layers not already present in the registry (deduplication). Use docker push --all-tags to push every tag for a repository. Buildx build --push combines build and push in one command.

sections.title: Pulling images
sections.body: Pull by tag for human-readable references or by digest for immutable pinning. Docker pull downloads only layers not already cached locally. Pull happens automatically on docker run if the image is not present. Use docker pull to pre-cache images before running.

sections.title: Multi-platform images
sections.body: Build for multiple architectures (amd64, arm64) with docker buildx build --platform. The result is a manifest list pointing to platform-specific image variants. Docker automatically selects the correct variant at pull time. Requires a buildx builder with docker-container or remote driver.

sections.title: CI/CD push patterns
sections.body: In GitHub Actions, use docker/login-action and docker/build-push-action for streamlined builds. In GitLab CI, use docker-in-docker services. Tag images with the commit SHA for traceability. Push to the registry on merge to main or on tag creation. Cache layers across CI runs with BuildKit cache export.

links.context: Parent skill for the full image lifecycle

related.note: Build precedes push in the image lifecycle

scripts.name: Push Image
scripts.description: Tag and push a Docker image to a container registry
scripts.inline: FULL_TAG="${REGISTRY}/${REPOSITORY}:${TAG}" && docker tag "${SOURCE_IMAGE}" "$FULL_TAG" && echo "Pushing $FULL_TAG ..." && docker push "$FULL_TAG" && echo '' && echo "Successfully pushed $FULL_TAG" && docker inspect --format 'Digest: {{index .RepoDigests 0}}' "$FULL_TAG"

scripts.name: Pull Image
scripts.description: Pull a Docker image from a container registry by tag or digest

IMAGE.description: Full image reference (e.g. ghcr.io/org/myapp:v1.0.0 or nginx@sha256:abc...)

scripts.name: Login to Registry
scripts.description: Authenticate with a container registry using token-based login
scripts.inline: echo "$REGISTRY_TOKEN" | docker login "$REGISTRY" -u "$REGISTRY_USER" --password-stdin && echo "Successfully authenticated with $REGISTRY"

scripts.name: Build and Push Multi-Platform
scripts.description: Build a multi-architecture image and push to a registry in one step
scripts.inline: PLATFORMS="${PLATFORMS:-linux/amd64,linux/arm64}" && FULL_TAG="${REGISTRY}/${REPOSITORY}:${TAG}" && echo "Building for $PLATFORMS and pushing as $FULL_TAG ..." && docker buildx build --platform "$PLATFORMS" --push ${DOCKERFILE_PATH:+-f "$DOCKERFILE_PATH"} -t "$FULL_TAG" "${BUILD_CONTEXT:-.}" && echo '' && echo "Successfully built and pushed multi-platform image $FULL_TAG" && docker manifest inspect "$FULL_TAG"

repository.description: Repository path within the registry (e.g. org/myapp)

platforms.description: Target platforms for multi-arch builds (e.g. linux/amd64, linux/arm64)

size.description: Compressed image size pushed to registry

depends.reason: Images must be built before they can be pushed

author: dojo-community
